title: NOC 65311 – Specialized cleaners | GTR Immigration
description: Specialized cleaners under NOC 65311 are the technical experts of the industrial and commercial cleaning world. Unlike light-duty cleaners, these TEER 5 professionals operate heavy-duty machinery and use specialized chemicals to clean carpets, upholstery, windows, and industrial equipment. In 2026, as Canada’s infrastructure and commercial real estate sectors expand, this role has become a high-demand trade-adjacent occupation. For immigration, it serves as an excellent pathway through the Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP) and various Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs) that value technical maintenance skills.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level5Major Group65 – Sales and service support occupationsSub-Major Group653 – Support occupations in personal and specialized servicesMinor Group6531 – Cleaning and environmental support occupationsNOC Code65311

duties: Operate industrial vacuum cleaners, high-pressure washers, and steam-cleaning equipment to clean carpets, rugs, and upholstered furniture.
Clean windows, glass partitions, and mirrors at ground level and on high-rise buildings using ladders, scaffolding, or specialized safety equipment.
Remove soot and creosote from chimneys and fireplace flues using specialized brushes and industrial vacuums.
Clean and disinfect industrial equipment, storage tanks, and large-scale ventilation systems.
Use chemical strippers and specialized waxes to restore and polish stone or wood surfaces.
Inspect work areas to ensure all safety and hygiene standards are met.
Provide estimates to customers for specialized cleaning services.
employment_requirements: No formal education is usually required, though some secondary school is often preferred.
On-the-job training is provided for the use of specialized machinery and chemicals.
WHMIS Certification: Mandatory for handling industrial cleaning chemicals.
Fall Protection/Working at Heights Training: Specifically required for window washers and chimney sweeps.
A valid driver’s license is often required for mobile service roles.
Physical stamina and the ability to work in varied environments (heights, confined spaces) are essential.
job_outlook: The outlook for specialized cleaning is Favourable. Projections indicate steady growth as property managers and industrial facility operators prioritize deep-cleaning protocols to maintain asset value and health standards. The retirement of seasoned industrial cleaners is creating a specialized labor gap that international workers are well-positioned to fill.
labour_market_demand: The demand for specialized cleaners is Good to Very Good. In 2026, there is a specific surge in demand for Industrial Cleaners and Window Washers in provinces like Ontario (GTA), Quebec, and British Columbia. As corporate offices and high-rise residential towers continue to dominate the skyline of cities like Toronto and Vancouver, the requirement for professional exterior and deep-cleaning services remains at an all-time high.
Technical Skill Set: Gain experience operating professional-grade steam cleaners, industrial vacuums, and high-pressure washers.
Higher Earning Potential: Due to the specialized nature of the work and potential for heights (window washing), wages are often higher than standard cleaning roles.
Niche Career Path: Excellent foundation for starting your own specialized cleaning business or moving into Cleaning Supervision (NOC 62024).
Accessible PR: A recognized occupation for the New 2026 TR to PR Pathway and regional immigration pilots.
pathways: Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A direct route for workers with job offers from designated employers in the Atlantic region.
BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled (ELSS): Accessible for those working in the service/maintenance sector for 9 consecutive months in BC.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Fast-track PR for those currently working in Alberta in eligible service categories.
OINP Employer Job Offer (In-Demand Skills): Targeted at specific maintenance roles that support Ontario’s corporate and industrial infrastructure.
